ChannelsListQuery.description: No inputs — lists all discovered channels with their enabled state.
ChannelsListQuery.title: ChannelsListQuery

ConfigGetQuery.description: No inputs — returns the full effective config + JSON schema.
ConfigGetQuery.title: ConfigGetQuery

ConfigGetResult.description:
Full effective config (secrets masked) plus its JSON schema.

Both fields are ``dict[str, Any]`` escape hatches: the config dict is
dynamically assembled by pydantic and the schema is generated by
``Config.model_json_schema``.

``json_schema`` is aliased to ``"schema"`` on the wire because ``schema``
is a reserved name on ``pydantic.BaseModel``.
ConfigGetResult.title: ConfigGetResult

CronListQuery.description: No inputs — lists all jobs including disabled ones.
CronListQuery.title: CronListQuery

ForgetResult.description:
Result of a SUCCESSFUL archive — ``result`` is always ``"archived"``.

The failure outcomes are raised as DomainErrors so the front door renders
them as problem+json (one error format): protected → ``ForbiddenError`` (403),
invalid → ``ValidationFailedError`` (422), not_found → ``NotFoundError`` (404).
ForgetResult.title: ForgetResult

ListTokensQuery.description: No inputs — list all token metadata.
ListTokensQuery.title: ListTokensQuery

MCPOAuthConfig.description:
OAuth settings for a remote MCP server.

Presence of an ``oauth`` value (``True`` or this object) marks the server
as OAuth-requiring. The SDK does dynamic client registration automatically;
``client_id`` / ``client_secret`` are an optional static-registration
override. ``scope`` is an optional requested-scope hint.
MCPOAuthConfig.title: MCPOAuthConfig

MCPSamplingConfig.description:
Governance for server-initiated ``sampling/createMessage`` (SP-6).

Sampling lets an MCP server ask durin's LLM to generate text. It is
**off by default** — a server only gains LLM access when the user opts
in. All limits below bound what a server can do once enabled.
MCPSamplingConfig.title: MCPSamplingConfig

MCPServerConfig.description: MCP server connection configuration (stdio or HTTP).
MCPServerConfig.title: MCPServerConfig

McpListQuery.description: No inputs — lists all configured servers.
McpListQuery.title: McpListQuery

McpRuntimeStatusResult.description: Whether the host can launch a registry server's local package right now.
McpRuntimeStatusResult.title: McpRuntimeStatusResult

McpServerUpsertCommand.description:
Create or replace a server. ``config`` is the full server config so the
webui form can edit every field (basic + advanced).
McpServerUpsertCommand.title: McpServerUpsertCommand

McpUpdatesQuery.description: No inputs — checks every configured server against the registry.
McpUpdatesQuery.title: McpUpdatesQuery

MemoryGraphQuery.description: No inputs — returns the full entity graph.
MemoryGraphQuery.title: MemoryGraphQuery

MemoryResult.description: Carries a raw graph-api payload dict (escape hatch).
MemoryResult.title: MemoryResult

OAuthPollResult.description:
Poll tick result.

``status`` is one of ``"pending"`` | ``"ok"`` | ``"error"``.
When ``status == "ok"`` the connected-session fields are also present.
OAuthPollResult.title: OAuthPollResult

OAuthStartLoopbackCommand.description:
Command for ``GET /api/oauth/codex/start-loopback``.

``is_local`` gates the loopback flow — the service raises ``ForbiddenError``
if False.
OAuthStartLoopbackCommand.title: OAuthStartLoopbackCommand

OAuthStatusQuery.description:
Query for ``GET /api/oauth/codex/status``.

``is_local`` — True when the HTTP request arrived via localhost — is used
to populate ``can_loopback`` in the response.
OAuthStatusQuery.title: OAuthStatusQuery

SecretStoreCommand.description:
Create or update a secret. An empty ``value`` is allowed ONLY as a
metadata-only edit of an existing secret (the stored credential is kept).
SecretStoreCommand.title: SecretStoreCommand

SecretsListQuery.description: No inputs — lists all secret metadata.
SecretsListQuery.title: SecretsListQuery

SessionsListQuery.description: No inputs — lists all websocket-channel sessions.
SessionsListQuery.title: SessionsListQuery

SettingsQuery.description: No inputs — returns the full settings payload.
SettingsQuery.title: SettingsQuery

SettingsResult.description:
Full settings payload.

``agent``, ``providers``, ``web_search``, and ``runtime`` mirror the keys
the existing handler returns.  The nested structures are ``dict[str, Any]``
escape hatches — open by design.
SettingsResult.title: SettingsResult

SkillsResult.description:
A successful skills response — ``data`` is the raw store payload.

The store returns ``(status, payload)``; a non-2xx status is raised as a
DomainError by :func:`_skills_result` (payload echoed in ``details``) so the
front door renders it as problem+json. Returned SkillsResults are 2xx only.
SkillsResult.title: SkillsResult
